join_order.probe: num.adsh via direct row index (adsh_code == sub_row_index)
join_order.structure: bool sub_passes[86135] + int32_t sub_sic[86135] + int32_t sub_cik[86135]
join_order.note: Direct array lookup O(1); adsh_code is sub row index

join_order.build: tag — pre-built tagver_hash.bin (mmap, zero build cost)
join_order.structure: mmap tagver_hash.bin: [{tag_code,ver_code,row_idx,valid}...]; probe gives row_idx → tag.abstract[row_idx], tag.tlabel[row_idx]
join_order.probing: bounded: for(uint64_t p=0; p<ht_size; p++) { slot=(h+p)&mask; if(!valid) break; if match return row_idx; }
join_order.note: P11: pre-built index eliminates build phase; C24: bounded probe loop

join_order.build: pre(stmt='EQ') → hash_map keyed on (adsh_code, tag_code, ver_code)
join_order.probing: bounded: for(uint64_t p=0; p<cap; p++) { slot=(h+p)&mask; if(!valid) break; if(k1==e.k1&&k2==e.k2) hit; }
join_order.value: none (existence check suffices; stmt is constant EQ_code)
join_order.note: C9: capacity=next_power_of_2(2*1.24M)=4194304; C24: bounded probe; EQ_code looked up from stmt_dict at runtime (C2)

filters.note: Lookup USD code from uom_dict at runtime (C2); check num_uom[i] == usd_code
Empirically 100% pass — skip !isnan check (or keep for safety at zero cost)

filters.applied_as: sub_passes[adsh_code] bool array check during num scan
pre hash table membership; only EQ rows inserted at build time

filters.note: Non-selective but required; check tag_abstract[row_idx] == 0 after pre-built index lookup

data_structures.sub_filter: bool sub_passes[86135] (stack or heap array); int32_t sub_sic[86135]; int32_t sub_cik[86135] — loaded once, read-only during probe
data_structures.tag_join: mmap pre-built tagver_hash.bin — zero build cost; layout: [uint64_t ht_size][{int32_t tag_code, int32_t ver_code, int32_t row_idx, int32_t valid}...]; access tag_abstract and tag_tlabel arrays by row_idx
data_structures.pre_join: open_addressing hash set; capacity=4194304 (next_power_of_2(1238503*2)); entry={uint64_t k1, uint64_t k2, bool valid}; ~100MB
data_structures.aggregation: thread_local open_addressing hash map; key=uint64_t ((uint32_t)sic<<32|(uint32_t)tlabel_code); value={double sum_val, int64_t cnt_val, open_addressing_hash_set<int32_t> distinct_ciks}; sized next_power_of_2(est_groups*2)

group_by_key.stmt_note: stmt is constant EQ_code for all surviving rows; include in output but not needed in hash key (C15 satisfied: all 3 GROUP BY columns deterministic)
group_by_key.correctness_c15: sic and tlabel_code uniquely identify group; stmt constant=EQ

aggregates.thread_local: int64_t cnt_val per group (for AVG = sum/cnt)

aggregates.structure: per-group open_addressing hash set of int32_t cik values in thread-local map; union across threads during merge
aggregates.merge: for each group key: iterate all threads, insert each thread's cik set into global set; final distinct count = global set size

parallelism.merge_strategy: sequential merge of thread-local maps into global map; union distinct_cik sets per group; acceptable since group count is small (~thousands)

indexes.purpose: join_lookup: (tag_code, ver_code) → tag row_idx in O(1); eliminates runtime hash table build for 1.07M tag rows
indexes.layout: [uint64_t ht_size][{int32_t tag_code, int32_t ver_code, int32_t row_idx, int32_t valid}...]

post_aggregation.having: COUNT(DISTINCT cik) >= 2  — filter groups after merge
post_aggregation.sort_algo: std::partial_sort on filtered groups (O(n log k), k=500)

output.decode_note: C18: decode tlabel_code via tlabel_dict[code] and stmt_code via stmt_dict[code] for output

correctness_checklist.C2: All dictionary codes (USD, EQ) looked up at runtime from dict files, never hardcoded
correctness_checklist.C9: pre_hash capacity = next_power_of_2(1238503*2) = 4194304; agg hash sized next_power_of_2(est_groups*2)
correctness_checklist.C15: Group key captures all 3 GROUP BY columns: sic, tlabel_code, stmt_code
correctness_checklist.C18: tlabel and stmt decoded via dict vectors for CSV output
correctness_checklist.C20: No memset for multi-byte sentinels; use std::fill or explicit loop initialization
correctness_checklist.C24: All hash table probes use bounded for-loop (probe < capacity), not unbounded while
